briefs.title: Council to vote on blocking BC Housing complex care project at 5389 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a motion that would commit the City to not support, approve, or endorse a BC Housing supportive and complex care housing development at 5389 Imperial Street. Councillor Lee brought forward the motion citing strong community opposition and concerns about the site's suitability and the adequacy of public consultation by BC Housing.
briefs.action_required: Attend the February 10 Council meeting to have your say before the vote.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — BC Housing Imperial Street complex care motion

briefs.title: Council to Consider Petition Opposing Supportive Housing Project at 5389 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Council will consider a supplementary petition from Burnaby Neighbourhood Watch — a follow-up to an earlier petition — signed by 981 residents opposing a proposed BC Housing supportive/complex care housing development at 5389 Imperial Street in Burnaby. The petition asks Council to reject the project. No decision has been made yet; Council is only being asked to formally receive and acknowledge the petition at the February 10, 2026 meeting.
briefs.action_required: Attend the February 10 Council meeting or submit your views if this project affects your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — petition on 5389 Imperial St housing project considered

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby's proposed 2026–2030 Five-Year Financial Plan
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would adopt the city's five-year financial plan covering 2026 to 2030. If approved, the plan would set out how the city expects to raise and spend money each year — with total proposed spending of roughly $1.35 billion in 2026 alone, funded mainly through property taxes (44.8%) and fees for city services.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — property tax and fee changes in this plan could affect your business costs.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026–2030 Financial Plan Bylaw — council adoption vote

briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Consider Update on Burnaby's Online Recreation Registration System Improvements
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive an information update on improvements made to WebReg, the city's online recreation registration system, since it launched in 2023. The update covers changes like rolling registration windows for swim and skate lessons, a new flexible monthly pass option, removal of annual pass holds, and better tools to ensure fair access for Burnaby residents.
briefs.action_required: If you use Burnaby recreation facilities, review the new pass options and updated registration rules at the City's website.

briefs.title: Council to consider: Burnaby's 10 park development projects planned for 2026–2027
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report outlining 10 park improvement projects planned for 2026–2027 in Burnaby. The projects — already included in the 2026–2030 capital plan — include playground replacements, a new basketball court, spray park design, artificial turf replacement, trail management, and signage upgrades across several parks.
briefs.action_required: Check the City of Burnaby project webpage and watch for community engagement surveys if your business is near an affected park.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan Moves to Drafting Phase
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive an update on the Parks, Recreation and Culture (PRC) Plan, which is now moving into Phase 3 — the drafting stage. A second round of public engagement is planned for March 2026, where residents can review and give feedback on the draft plan before it is finalized later in spring 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the March 2026 public engagement — attend an open house or complete the online survey to have your say.

key_dates.label: Burnaby PRC Plan Phase 4 public engagement opens — comment period begins

briefs.title: Council to Receive Burnaby Building Permit Activity Report for December 2025
briefs.summary: Council will consider receiving a routine information report on building permit activity in Burnaby for December 2025. The report shows 132 permits were issued that month, representing $152.9 million in construction value, and that 2025 as a whole saw 1,225 permits totalling over $3.15 billion — up significantly from prior years.
briefs.action_required: Check the City of Burnaby website after Feb 10, 2026 for the published permit activity data.

briefs.title: Council to vote on: $10,000 for staff to attend 2026 BCRPA Symposium in Vancouver
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on spending up to $10,000 to send up to six members of the Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee to the 2026 BCRPA Symposium in Vancouver, running April 28 – May 1, 2026. The BCRPA (British Columbia Recreation and Parks Association) is a non-profit group that supports parks and recreation across the province, and this is its main annual event.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $380,000 contract renewal for road marking paint and glass beads
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on renewing a supply contract with Cloverdale Paint Inc. for road marking paint and glass beads — materials used to keep lane lines and pavement markings visible across the city. The proposed two-year renewal (March 2026 to February 2028) would cost an estimated $380,000 including taxes, with no price increase per unit compared to the previous term.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on February 10, 2026 — this affects road supply contracts in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby road marking paint contract renewal — council vote
Burnaby road marking paint contract renewal period begins

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Renewing Burnaby's temporary fence rental contract for $267,000
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing its temporary fence rental contract with Super Save Fence Rentals Inc. for one final year (June 1, 2026 to May 31, 2027) at an estimated cost of $267,000 including taxes. The fencing supports city events, construction sites, and public safety needs — and the supplier has agreed to hold prices steady for this final term.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if your business supplies fencing or event services to the City of Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fence rental contract renewal — proposed start date
Burnaby fence rental contract renewal — proposed end date

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on $7.3M renewal of Burnaby's police dispatch contract with E-COMM
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing its police dispatch services contract with E-COMM Emergency Communications for BC Incorporated for one year (January 1 to December 31, 2026) at an estimated cost of $7,300,000. The renewal — a roughly 1% increase over last year — would keep the same contract terms in place while the City continues reviewing whether E-COMM's service levels meet its needs.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision on February 10, 2026 — this affects public safety service funding in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — E-COMM police dispatch contract renewal approval

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to approve $240,000 contract increase for hazardous building material surveys
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a $240,000 contract change (an increase to an existing agreement) with Astech Consultants Ltd. to continue pre-demolition hazardous building material surveys at City-owned properties. The extra funding is needed because more houses are being demolished, requiring more inspections for dangerous materials like asbestos before work begins.
briefs.action_required: If you do demolition or environmental consulting work for the City, watch for related contract opportunities.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — hazardous building material contract change vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on $193,000 contract change for senior housekeeping program
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a $193,000 contract change (an increase to an existing agreement) with Comfort Keepers to keep delivering light housekeeping services to seniors under the Better at Home Program through December 31, 2026. The program is funded by a BC government grant through United Way and helps seniors live independently at home — costs are fully covered by that funding and fees paid by participating seniors.
briefs.action_required: If you provide home support or senior care services, watch this contract decision — it signals ongoing city demand.

key_dates.label: Better at Home housekeeping contract change — service end date

briefs.title: Council to vote on $700,000 contract extension for archaeological consulting services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a $700,000 contract change — meaning an increase to an existing contract — with Campfire Archaeology and Heritage Ltd. If approved, the firm would continue providing archaeological consulting services to support City construction projects, help Burnaby comply with BC's Heritage Conservation Act, and advance the City's commitments to Truth and Reconciliation with Indigenous Peoples.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect construction timelines if your projects require archaeological review.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — archaeological consulting contract change vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on buying 3 more Toyota Tacoma trucks for $174,384
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a contract change that would add three Toyota Tacoma pickup trucks to an existing deal with OpenRoad Toyota Richmond, at an estimated cost of $174,384 including taxes. The trucks would replace three aging city vehicles, which would then be sold at auction, and the purchase would be funded through the city's Equipment Replacement Reserve — a dedicated fund set aside to swap out old vehicles and machinery.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $20M fuel supply contract with 4Refuel Canada LP
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a five-year contract worth up to $20 million (including taxes) to 4Refuel Canada LP for the supply and delivery of gasoline, diesel, biodiesel, and related fuel products to City facilities. The contract would run from March 11, 2026 to March 10, 2031, with the option to extend for two additional one-year periods if service and pricing remain satisfactory.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on February 10, 2026 — this affects City fuel supply operations.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fuel supply contract with 4Refuel Canada LP proposed start date

briefs.title: Council to vote on $648,206 contract for new Burnaby garbage truck
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth $648,206 (including taxes) to Rollins Machinery Ltd. for the purchase of a new tandem axle front load refuse truck — a large commercial garbage truck used for curbside collection. The new truck would replace an older unit, which the City plans to sell at auction once the replacement arrives.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on February 10, 2026 — this affects waste collection services in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — refuse truck contract award decision

briefs.title: Council to vote on $621,838 contract for two new split body refuse trucks
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth $621,838 (including taxes) to Rollins Machinery Ltd. for the purchase of two Ford/HaulAll split body refuse trucks. These trucks would replace two aging city vehicles, which would then be sold at auction. The funding would come from the city's Equipment Replacement Reserve Fund.
briefs.action_required: No action required — this is a city equipment purchase with no direct impact on most businesses.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Permanently Closing Lochdale School Park Junior Playground
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to permanently decommission the junior playground at Lochdale School Park and delay its replacement until future budget cycles. The playground was closed in December 2025 after it became unsafe and no longer met Canadian Standards Association safety rules. Staff recommend deferring any replacement rather than redirecting funds from other planned park projects.
briefs.action_required: If you use or rely on this playground, attend the February 10, 2026 Council meeting to share your input.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Lochdale School Park playground decision

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby Procedure Bylaw update for advisory body oversight
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on amendments to the Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024 that would change how the rules (called 'Terms of Reference') for the City's advisory bodies are created and updated. Under the proposed changes, the Mayor would direct the preparation of these rules, and a review committee would check them before they go to Council for approval. Public notice is required before the bylaw can be adopted.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public notice posting — you can review the proposed changes on Burnaby's website under Public Notices.

briefs.title: Council to vote on removing restaurant-only restriction at Burnaby industrial site
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on removing a legal restriction (called a Section 219 covenant — a rule registered on a property's title that limits how it can be used) at 8340 Fraser Reach Court. The restriction currently requires a 2,217 sq. ft. space in an industrial warehouse to be used as a restaurant only. Since no restaurant tenant has been found since the building opened in 2018, staff are recommending the restriction be lifted so the space can be used for light industrial purposes instead.
briefs.action_required: If you are interested in leasing this space as a restaurant, contact the property owner before the February 10 council vote.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — proposed covenant discharge at 8340 Fraser Reach Court

briefs.industries: Food Service, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on five new non-residential zoning districts
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on proposed changes to Burnaby's Zoning Bylaw that would create five new zoning districts — E1 (Employment), I1 (Industrial), PM1 (Parks & Open Spaces), PM2 (Institutional), and AM1 (Agriculture) — to put the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (the city's long-term land use blueprint) into effect. If approved, the new rules would change what types of businesses and uses are allowed on affected properties, including new conditions on storage facilities, housing near SkyTrain stations, and heavy industrial uses.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property falls under a new district — the new rules could change what your business is allowed to do.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — new non-residential zoning districts bylaw

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Agriculture

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Vancouver Fraser Port Authority Update on Second Narrows Dredging Works
briefs.summary: The Vancouver Fraser Port Authority is set to appear before Burnaby Mayor and Council on February 10, 2026, to give an update on dredging works at Second Narrows. Dredging means removing sediment from the bottom of a waterway to keep it deep enough for ships. Representatives will give a short slide presentation and answer questions from Council.
briefs.action_required: Attend the February 10, 2026 Council meeting if the Second Narrows dredging works affect your business.

key_dates.label: Vancouver Fraser Port Authority Second Narrows dredging update — Burnaby Council delegation

briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Natural Resources, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves 2026–2030 Financial Plan and multiple city contracts
briefs.summary: At its January 27, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on a range of items including the city's five-year financial plan, several construction and service contracts, housing agreement bylaws, and a bike learning zone project. Most items passed unanimously, though the financial plan bylaw faced one opposing vote. These decisions affect city spending, infrastructure, and housing in Burnaby through 2030.
briefs.action_required: Review the 2026–2030 Financial Plan for any changes to city fees or services that may affect your business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Rewrite to Reshape Rules for Employment, Industrial & Agricultural Land
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a major rewrite of its Zoning Bylaw — the rulebook that controls what businesses can operate where. The proposed changes would cut the number of zoning categories from 53 down to 15, add new rules for self-storage facilities near SkyTrain stations, allow limited housing on employment lands near transit, and update regulations for farms, parks, schools, and industrial sites.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed zoning changes at burnaby.ca to see if your business location or land use could be affected.
